title: malache wrightii
intro: Malache wrightii, also known as Wright's malachite or Wright's buckthorn, is a shrub or small tree native to the southeastern United States. It is a member of the Rutaceae family, which includes citrus and rue plants, and is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. Malache wrightii can be prepared into herbal remedies such as tinctures, teas, and infusions, which can be used to harness its health benefits.
